message:
fix(tui): derive open-blockers count from the live graph, not the stale metrics cache

Two TUI list paths were reading `self.analyzer.metrics.blocked_by_count`
to display the number of open blockers per issue. That cache is computed
once during analyzer initialization and is never updated as the TUI
mutates the graph (claim/close/release operations, filter toggles that
re-scope the visible issue set, etc.). Result: after the user closed a
blocker from the TUI, the surrounding list still showed the pre-close
blocked-by count until a full re-analysis — a confusing staleness.

Replace both call sites with `self.analyzer.graph.open_blockers(&issue.id).len()`,
which walks the current dependency graph and counts only the blockers
that are still open right now. `open_blockers` is already used elsewhere
in the TUI for the detail view, so the two list views now agree with
the detail view on the same metric.

- `blocked_by_count` cache and its two callers are the only consumers
  in the TUI that were out of sync; other callers (the JSON export,
  the analytics overlay) already query the graph live.
- No perf regression: `open_blockers` is O(in-degree) per issue and
  the TUI rendering pass only calls it for the currently visible
  window (page-sized).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
